# Sub PEQ filters
# miniDSP-style peaking filters plus normalized biquad coefficients
# Biquad convention: y[n] = b0*x[n] + b1*x[n-1] + b2*x[n-2] - a1*y[n-1] - a2*y[n-2]

Filter 1: ON PK Fc 179.810 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 6.6667
  biquad b0=1.00030613559 b1=-1.99837744782 b2=0.9982097057 a1=-1.99837744782 a2=0.998515841294
Filter 2: ON PK Fc 57.861 Hz Gain -5.626 dB Q 4.2294
  biquad b0=0.999705083863 b1=-1.99874855836 b2=0.999057807075 a1=-1.99874855836 a2=0.998762890938
Filter 3: ON PK Fc 106.567 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 3.6344
  biquad b0=1.0003327946 b1=-1.99833798862 b2=0.998053802692 a1=-1.99833798862 a2=0.998386597289
Filter 4: ON PK Fc 46.875 Hz Gain +2.771 dB Q 2.2584
  biquad b0=1.0002175022 b1=-1.99883313142 b2=0.998625036157 a1=-1.99883313142 a2=0.998842538353

  biquad b0=0.999846545717 b1=-1.99893810618 b2=0.999119604379 a1=-1.99893810618 a2=0.998966150096

  biquad b0=0.999622703689 b1=-1.99723664546 b2=0.997701203665 a1=-1.99723664546 a2=0.997323907354
